constraints.expression: consumer recomputes pack_hash via safeCanonicalize(pack-minus-pack_hash) and rejects the record on mismatch
constraints.message: pack_hash MUST equal SHA-256 over safeCanonicalize(pack-minus-pack_hash). The library does NOT recompute or compare; consumer-side reconciliation is required.
constraints.evaluation_note: Content-addressed-hash reconciliation is consumer-side per ADR-010 (class-vs-policy boundary). The schema enforces the sha256:<64-hex> format via the field pattern; the library evaluator does NOT recompute the canonical-JSON form of the pack body and compare against pack_hash. Consumers MUST: (1) run safeCanonicalize() over the pack object minus the pack_hash field; (2) compute SHA-256 of the canonical bytes; (3) reject the record when the computed digest differs from pack_hash. The 100KB normative payload cap (per docs/architecture/hashing-spec-freeze-v8.5.md) applies; safeCanonicalize() throws when the canonical form exceeds the cap. Surfaced via the UnverifiedObligationsManifest as INTEGRITY_DEFERRED when the consumer opts in to shape-only validation.
